title: meibomia leiocarpa var. subsecunda
intro: Meibomia leiocarpa var. subsecunda, also known as the subsecunda meibomia or woodland meadow pea, is a perennial legume native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of coughs and colds. The health benefits of Meibomia leiocarpa var. subsecunda include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
